metadata.summary: Parallel test runner for Robot Framework

documentation: A parallel executor for Robot Framework tests. With Pabot you can split one execution into multiple and save test execution time.

methods.name: pabot.SharedLibrary.SharedLibrary.__init__
documentation:
Import a library so that the library instance is shared between executions.
[https://pabot.org/PabotLib.html?ref=log#import-shared-library|Open online docs.]

methods.name: pabot.pabotlib.PabotLib.set_polling_seconds
documentation: Determine the amount of seconds to wait between checking for free locks. Default: 0.1  (100ms)

methods.name: pabot.pabotlib.PabotLib.set_polling_seconds_setupteardown
documentation: Determine the amount of seconds to wait between checking for free locks during setup and teardown. Default: 0.3  (300ms)

methods.name: pabot.pabotlib.PabotLib.set_polling_logging
documentation: Enable or disable logging inside of polling. Logging inside of polling can be disabled (enable=False) to reduce log file size.

methods.name: pabot.pabotlib.PabotLib.run_setup_only_once
documentation:
Runs a keyword only once at the first possible moment when
an execution has gone through this step.
[https://pabot.org/PabotLib.html?ref=log#run-setup-only-once|Open online docs.]

methods.name: pabot.pabotlib.PabotLib.run_only_once
documentation:
Runs a keyword only once in one of the parallel processes. Optional arguments of the keyword needs to be serializeable in order to
create an unique lockname.
Sample request sequence [keyword, keyword 'x', keyword, keyword 5, keyword 'x', keyword 5]
results in execution of [keyword, keyword 'x', keyword 5]
[https://pabot.org/PabotLib.html?ref=log#run-only-once|Open online docs.]

methods.name: pabot.pabotlib.PabotLib.run_teardown_only_once
documentation:
Runs a keyword only once after all executions have gone through this step in the last possible moment.
[https://pabot.org/PabotLib.html?ref=log#run-teardown-only-once|Open online docs.]

methods.name: pabot.pabotlib.PabotLib.run_on_last_process
documentation:
Runs a keyword only on last process used by pabot.
[https://pabot.org/PabotLib.html?ref=log#run-on-last-process|Open online docs.]

methods.name: pabot.pabotlib.PabotLib.set_parallel_value_for_key
documentation:
Set a globally available key and value that can be accessed
from all the pabot processes.
[https://pabot.org/PabotLib.html?ref=log#set-parallel-value-for-key|Open online docs.]

methods.name: pabot.pabotlib.PabotLib.get_parallel_value_for_key
documentation:
Get the value for a key. If there is no value for the key then empty
string is returned.
[https://pabot.org/PabotLib.html?ref=log#get-parallel-value-for-key|Open online docs.]

methods.name: pabot.pabotlib.PabotLib.acquire_lock
documentation:
Wait for a lock with name.
[https://pabot.org/PabotLib.html?ref=log#acquire-lock|Open online docs.]

methods.name: pabot.pabotlib.PabotLib.release_lock
documentation:
Release a lock with name.
[https://pabot.org/PabotLib.html?ref=log#release-lock|Open online docs.]

methods.name: pabot.pabotlib.PabotLib.release_locks
documentation:
Release all locks called by instance.
[https://pabot.org/PabotLib.html?ref=log#release-locks|Open online docs.]

methods.name: pabot.pabotlib.PabotLib.acquire_value_set
documentation:
Reserve a set of values for this execution.
[https://pabot.org/PabotLib.html?ref=log#acquire-value-set|Open online docs.]

methods.name: pabot.pabotlib.PabotLib.get_value_from_set
documentation:
Get a value from previously reserved value set.
[https://pabot.org/PabotLib.html?ref=log#get-value-from-set|Open online docs.]

methods.name: pabot.pabotlib.PabotLib.release_value_set
documentation:
Release a reserved value set so that other executions can use it also.
[https://pabot.org/PabotLib.html?ref=log#release-value-set|Open online docs.]

methods.name: pabot.pabotlib.PabotLib.disable_value_set
documentation:
Disable a reserved value set.
[https://pabot.org/PabotLib.html?ref=log#disable-value-set|Open online docs.]

functions.name: pabot.robotremoteserver.test_remote_server
documentation:
Test is remote server running.

```
param uri

Server address.

param log

Log status message or not.
```

\:return `True` if server is running, `False` otherwise.

functions.name: pabot.robotremoteserver.stop_remote_server
documentation:
Stop remote server unless server has disabled stopping.

```
param uri

Server address.

param log

Log status message or not.
```

:return `True` if server was stopped or it was not running in

: the first place, `False` otherwise.

methods.name: pabot.robotremoteserver.RobotRemoteServer.__init__
documentation:
Configure and start-up remote server.

```
param library

Test library instance or module to host.

param host

Address to listen. Use '0.0.0.0' to listen
to all available interfaces.

param port

Port to listen. Use 0 to select a free port
automatically. Can be given as an integer or as
a string.

param port_file

File to write the port that is used. None means
no such file is written. Port file is created after
the server is started and removed automatically
after it has stopped.

param allow_stop

DEPRECATED since version 1.1. Use
allow_remote_stop instead.

param serve

If True, start the server automatically and
wait for it to be stopped.

param allow_remote_stop

Allow/disallow stopping the server using
Stop Remote Server keyword and
stop_remote_server XML-RPC method.
```

methods.name: pabot.robotremoteserver.RobotRemoteServer.server_address
documentation: Server address as a tuple `(host, port)`.

methods.name: pabot.robotremoteserver.RobotRemoteServer.server_port
documentation:
Server port as an integer.

If the initial given port is 0, also this property returns 0 until
the server is activated.

methods.name: pabot.robotremoteserver.RobotRemoteServer.activate
documentation:
Bind port and activate the server but do not yet start serving.

:return  Port number that the server is going to use. This is the
         actual port to use, even if the initially given port is 0.

methods.name: pabot.robotremoteserver.RobotRemoteServer.serve
documentation:
Start the server and wait for it to be stopped.

```
param log

When True, print messages about start and stop to
the console.
```

Automatically activates the server if it is not activated already.

If this method is executed in the main thread, automatically registers
signals SIGINT, SIGTERM and SIGHUP to stop the server.

Using this method requires using `serve=False` when initializing the
server. Using `serve=True` is equal to first using `serve=False`
and then calling this method.

In addition to signals, the server can be stopped with the `Stop
Remote Server` keyword and the `stop_remote_serve` XML-RPC method,
unless they are disabled when the server is initialized. If this method
is executed in a thread, then it is also possible to stop the server
using the {meth}`stop` method.

methods.name: pabot.robotremoteserver.RobotRemoteServer.stop
documentation: Stop server.

methods.name: pabot.robotremoteserver.RobotRemoteServer.stop_remote_server
documentation: Exposed XML-RPC methods. Should they be moved to own class?

modules.name: pabot.pabot
documentation:
A parallel executor for Robot Framework test cases.
Version [PABOT_VERSION]

Supports all Robot Framework command line options and also following
options (these must be before normal RF options):

--verbose
  more output

--command [ACTUAL COMMANDS TO START ROBOT EXECUTOR] --end-command
  RF script for situations where pybot is not used directly

--processes [NUMBER OF PROCESSES]
  How many parallel executors to use (default max of 2 and cpu count).
  Special option "all" will use as many processes as there are
  executable suites or tests.

--testlevelsplit
  Split execution on test level instead of default suite level.
  If .pabotsuitenames contains both tests and suites then this
  will only affect new suites and split only them.
  Leaving this flag out when both suites and tests in
  .pabotsuitenames file will also only affect new suites and
  add them as suite files.

--resourcefile [FILEPATH]
  Indicator for a file that can contain shared variables for
  distributing resources.

--pabotlib
  Start PabotLib remote server. This enables locking and resource
  distribution between parallel test executions.

--pabotlibhost [HOSTNAME]
  Host name of the PabotLib remote server (default is 127.0.0.1)

--pabotlibport [PORT]
  Port number of the PabotLib remote server (default is 8270)

--processtimeout [TIMEOUT]
  Maximum time in seconds to wait for a process before killing it. If not set, there's no timeout.

--ordering [FILE PATH]
  Optionally give execution order from a file.

--suitesfrom [FILEPATH TO OUTPUTXML]
  Optionally read suites from output.xml file. Failed suites will run
  first and longer running ones will be executed before shorter ones.

--argumentfile[INTEGER] [FILEPATH]
  Run same suite with multiple argumentfile options.
  For example "--argumentfile1 arg1.txt --argumentfile2 arg2.txt".

--shard [SHARD]/[SHARD COUNT]
  Optionally split execution into smaller pieces. This can
  be used for distributing testing to multiple machines.

--chunk
  Optionally chunk tests to PROCESSES number of robot runs.

Copyright 2022 Mikko Korpela - Apache 2 License

functions.name: pabot.pabot.outputxml_preprocessing
documentation: optionally invoke rebot for output.xml preprocessing to get --RemoveKeywords
and --flattenkeywords applied => result: much smaller output.xml files + faster merging + avoid MemoryErrors
